comment: Both name the receiver-side, currently-held perception of a brand. SBT's perception-cloud is an explicitly distributional object (a cloud of convictions across a population, with valence); Aaker's brand-image is the organized set of associations a customer holds. Close, not exact: SBT models the distribution and its variance; Aaker treats image as an aggregate association set. Documented SBT rename boundary: "perception cloud" not "brand image".

SBT's brand-conviction is an observer-relative held position on spectral dimensions (explicitly NOT dispositional). Incumbent (Aaker-era) "brand attitude" is the dispositional evaluative tendency toward a brand. Same functional slot (the held stance a signal collapses into) but opposed on the dispositional/observer-relative axis. Documented SBT rename boundary: "brand conviction" not "brand attitude". NOTE: aaker:brand-attitude is the Aaker-ERA incumbent term, not a key owned in the Aaker module above (attitude is the consumer-behavior tradition Aaker writes within); recorded here as the cross-walk target.

SBT cohorts are PERCEPTUAL groupings of observers by spectral profile, with dynamic membership; demographics are metadata not mechanism. Incumbent "market segment" is a demographic/behavioral division of a market. Related (both partition the audience) but mechanistically distinct: perceptual vs demographic basis. Documented SBT rename boundary: "cohort" not "segment". NOTE: aaker:market-segment is the incumbent segmentation tradition, not an owned Aaker-module key.

SBT's re-collapse names the STRUCTURAL MECHANISM by which a signal is re-emitted and re-collapsed into a changed perception cloud; incumbent "rebranding" is the conventional surface label for the managerial activity. Close match on referent (the event of a brand changing its perceived form) but SBT names the mechanism while the incumbent names the practice. Documented SBT rename boundary: "re-collapse" not "rebranding". NOTE: aaker:rebranding is the incumbent practitioner term, not an owned Aaker-module key.

SBT's coherence-type is a STRUCTURAL CLASSIFICATION of brands by the geometric pattern of emission across the eight dimensions (a type, not a score; ordering predicts crisis resilience). Incumbent "brand consistency" is the managerial prescription that brand expressions agree over time and touchpoints (a scalar virtue). Related (both concern internal agreement of brand expression) but SBT typologizes geometry while the incumbent prescribes a scalar. NOTE: aaker:brand-consistency is an incumbent managerial value, not an owned Aaker-module key.

Both are DIMENSIONAL MODELS of a brand, and this is the cleanest case of an OWNED-key cross-walk: aaker:brand-personality IS owned in the Aaker module (the 5 dimensions: sincerity, excitement, competence, sophistication, ruggedness). SBT's spectral-dimensions are 8 PERCEPTUAL channels (semiotic, narrative, ideological, experiential, social, economic, cultural, temporal). Related (both decompose a brand into a fixed dimensional basis) but DIFFERENT dimensional models -- different count, different axes, different referent (perceptual channels vs human-trait analogues). relatedMatch, not closeMatch: the bases do not align term-for-term.
